title: Air-fryer roast carrots
subtitle: Honey-glazed carrots, caramelised fast.
excerpt: Carrots halved lengthways, tossed in oil, honey and thyme, and cooked in the air fryer at 190°C until the cut faces caramelise and the flesh turns tender. Twenty-five minutes. Serves two as a side.
sourceNotes: Standard air-fryer roasting technique applied to carrots; the honey glaze is a conventional British side-dish treatment.

content.text: Halved lengthways so the flat face sits against the basket floor. A little honey in the oil for caramelisation. A sprig of thyme for the smell. The air fryer at 190°C does in 22 minutes what a roasting tin at 220°C takes 35 to 40 to achieve. The result is a carrot with charred edges and a properly sweet, dense interior.

Preheat the air fryer to 190°C. Toss {{carrot}} halved carrots in a bowl with {{olive-oil}} of olive oil, {{honey}} of honey and {{sea-salt-fine}} of salt. Add the thyme sprigs if using. Make sure each carrot half is well coated.

Arrange the carrots cut-side down in the basket in a single layer. Cook for 20 to 22 minutes until the cut faces are caramelised and deep amber, and the thick end of each carrot yields to a knife with only slight resistance.

items.fix: Always place cut-side down against the basket floor. Increase to 200°C for the last 3 minutes.
items.cause: Cut-side was facing up, or temperature too low.
items.symptom: Carrots not caramelised after cooking time

items.fix: Use medium carrots and halve lengthways. If very large, cut into quarters.
items.symptom: Carrots burnt on outside but hard inside

items.fix: Stick to one tablespoon per four carrots. Most non-stick baskets release cleanly after cooking.
items.cause: Too much honey or basket not non-stick.

content.text: Honey-glazed carrots sit alongside a Sunday roast or a piece of grilled lamb. They also work cold the next day: slice and toss with rocket, a splash of sherry vinegar and crumbled goat's cheese for a quick salad. The sweetness of the carrot deepens overnight.
